message: Hey everyone, good morning! 🌞
Morning! Did anyone finish the assignment?
I stayed up till 3am working on it 😭
Same here, the database part was so confusing
I just submitted mine, finally done
Can someone share the notes from yesterday's lecture?
I have them, let me upload to Google Drive
Here's the link: https://drive.google.com/notes-ml-lecture-7
Thanks Aman! You're a lifesaver
By the way, when is the next exam?
I think it's on March 25th, Data Structures
Yeah March 25, and then AI on March 28
We should start a study group for DS
Good idea. Let's meet at the library tomorrow?
Works for me. What time?
How about 4pm?
I have a lab till 4:30, can we do 5?
5pm works, I'll book a study room
Perfect. Don't forget to bring your laptops
Should we prepare any specific topics?
Let's focus on trees and graphs first
And sorting algorithms, those always come in exams
Agreed. I'll bring the previous year question papers
Has anyone checked the exam schedule PDF?
Yeah it's on the university portal under announcements
Here's the direct link: https://university.edu/exam-schedule-spring-2026.pdf
Thanks! Downloading it now
Btw does anyone want to grab lunch later?
Sure, the new cafeteria has great pasta
I'm in! Let's go at 12:30
Count me in too 🍕
Guys, Prof. Sharma just posted the project guidelines
Oh finally! What's the topic?
We need to build a machine learning model for any real-world problem
That's pretty open-ended. Any ideas?
How about a recommendation system?
Or maybe sentiment analysis on social media
I was thinking image classification for medical diagnosis
That sounds cool! We could use the chest X-ray dataset
Let's discuss this in our meeting tomorrow
Speaking of meetings, the call is at 6pm today right?
Yes, 6pm. I'll send the Zoom link
Here you go: https://zoom.us/j/meeting-abc123
Got it, see you all at 6
Let's sync up in the evening then
I'll prepare a quick presentation about our project idea
Can you also share the research papers you mentioned?
Sure, here are the papers: https://arxiv.org/ml-papers-collection
Has anyone started the coding part yet?
I set up the GitHub repo yesterday
https://github.com/team-project/ml-classifier
Nice! I'll push my data preprocessing code tonight
I can work on the model architecture this weekend
I'll handle the dataset collection and cleaning
Great teamwork! We should have weekly standup calls
Monday evenings work? Like a quick 30-min sync
Monday evenings are fine for me
Same. Let's keep it regular
Anyone know if the hackathon registration is still open?
Yeah it closes on Friday. We should sign up!
OMG yes! The prizes are insane this year 🏆
What's the theme?
AI for social good. Perfect for us!
I'll register our team. What should our team name be?
How about 'Neural Ninjas'? 😂
lol that's actually good
I vote for 'Gradient Gangsters' 😎
Let's go with Neural Ninjas, sounds epic
Has anyone tried the new ChatGPT update?
Yeah the code generation is so much better now
I used it to debug my Python script yesterday, saved me hours
But remember, we can't use AI-generated code for the exam
Obviously lol, just for practice
Quick reminder: assignment submission deadline is tonight at 11:59 PM
Oh shoot I forgot to add the references section!
Make sure to follow the APA format, Prof Singh is strict about it
I can share my reference template if you want
Yes please! That would be super helpful
Shared on the drive: https://drive.google.com/reference-template-apa
Guys let's not forget the coding bootcamp this Saturday
What's the topic for this week?
Full stack web development with React and Node.js
Nice! I've been wanting to learn React
Is it virtual or in-person?
Hybrid. You can join the Google Meet if you want
Meet link: https://meet.google.com/bootcamp-week4
I'll attend in person, need to get out of my room 😅
The weather is nice today, perfect for a walk after lunch
True! Should we walk to the cafeteria instead of driving?
Definitely, it's only 10 minutes away
I just discovered a shortcut through the garden area
Oh nice, lead the way when we go!
Guys, the placement drive dates are out!
Which companies?
Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and a few startups
Google is coming?! When?!
April 10th. DS and Algo round first, then system design
We need to grind LeetCode starting now
I've been doing 2 problems a day for the past month
Share your LeetCode profile? Want to compare progress
https://leetcode.com/rahul_codes
Impressive streak! 45 days?!
OK heading to lunch now, anyone ready?
Yep let's go! 🍔
Coming in 2 mins, need to save my code
Meet at the main entrance
That pasta was amazing omg
Right?! I'm going back there tomorrow for sure
The garlic bread was chef's kiss 🤌
OK back to work. Who's doing what this afternoon?
I'm going to work on the database schema for our project
I'll write the data cleaning scripts
I need to finish reading chapter 8 for tomorrow's class
I'll look into the dataset options on Kaggle
Found a great dataset: https://kaggle.com/chest-xray-dataset
How many images?
About 5000 labeled images, 2 classes: normal and pneumonia
That's a good size for our project
Can we add more classes? Like different types of diseases?
We can merge with other datasets for multi-class classification
Don't forget to document everything in the project report
True. Let's use Google Docs for the report
Created the doc: https://docs.google.com/project-report-ml
Added to bookmark 👍
I'll start writing the introduction section tonight
Anyone watching the cricket match tonight? 🏏
India vs Australia! Can't miss it
I'll stream it while coding lol
Multitasking king 👑
LOL the score is gonna distract you
Hey the TA just posted office hours for tomorrow
What time?
2pm to 4pm in Room 301
Good, I have doubts about the ER diagram assignment
Me too. Should we go together?
Yes! Let's meet outside Room 301 at 2
I just realized we have a quiz on Wednesday. Did you all know?
WHAT?! In which subject?
Computer Networks. Chapter 4 and 5
Oh no, I haven't even started chapter 5
Don't panic, I have summary notes for both chapters
Here: https://drive.google.com/cn-ch4-ch5-summary
You're literally saving us every time 🙏
We should have a quick revision session tonight after the Zoom call
Good idea. We can do a quick call at 7:30
Let's sync tonight over a video call then
Perfect. So 6pm project meeting, then 7:30 CN revision
Someone remind me about the library book I need to return
Which book?
Design Patterns by Gang of Four
Oh I want to borrow that after you! Is it good?
Amazing. It changed how I think about code architecture
I just finished 'Clean Code' by Robert Martin, highly recommend
Added to my reading list 📚
Hey quick question - has anyone configured VS Code for Python debugging?
Yeah, you need to install the Python extension and set up launch.json
I can help you set it up if you want
That would be great! Maybe after our calls tonight?
Sure, I'll screen share and walk you through it
The project submission deadline is April 15th btw
That's only a month away! We need to speed up
Let's create a Gantt chart for the project timeline
Good idea. I'll make one in Google Sheets
We should also set up CI/CD for the project repo
I can handle that. GitHub Actions should work fine
Don't forget to add the .gitignore for Python and Jupyter files
Also add the requirements.txt with all dependencies
Will do. I'll set up the virtual environment too
Who's bringing snacks for tomorrow's study session?
I'll bring chips and cold drinks
I'll get samosas from the canteen
I'll bring cookies 🍪
Great, I'll bring coffee ☕
This study session is turning into a party 😂
Study hard, party harder 💪
Guys I can't find the lecture recording from last Friday
It should be on the course portal under recordings
Try this link: https://portal.university.edu/recordings/ml-lecture-5
Found it! Thanks Rahul
The recording is 2 hours long tho 😩
Watch it at 1.5x speed, works like a charm
1.75x or nothing 😤
Anyway, about our discussion at 6pm - can everyone make it?
Yep, I'll be there
Joining from my phone, but yes
I'll be on time. Let's keep it under 45 minutes

Agreed. Here's the agenda:
1. Project topic finalization
2. Task distribution
3. Timeline discussion
Good agenda. I vote for the X-ray classification project
Same, it has a strong dataset and clear problem statement
Let's finalize it on the call then
Joining the Zoom now, see you all there!
Zoom link isn't working for me 😫
Try refreshing, it worked after I refreshed
Or use the meeting ID directly: 123-456-7890
Got in! My internet is just slow today
Great call everyone! So we're going with the medical image classifier
Yes! Tasks are clear. I'll send the summary on the doc
Deadline for first prototype: March 30
I'll start on the CNN model architecture tonight
Ready for CN revision? Let's jump on the 7:30 call
Almost ready, finishing up dinner
I'll start the Google Meet: https://meet.google.com/cn-revision
Joining, let me grab my notes first
Chapter 4: Transport Layer. Who wants to explain TCP handshake?
I'll do it! SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK - the three-way handshake
Don't forget about TCP vs UDP differences, that's always on the quiz
TCP is reliable, connection-oriented. UDP is faster but no guarantee
Also important: flow control vs congestion control
Good revision session! I feel more confident about Wednesday now
Same! Let's do one more session tomorrow night?
Yes please! Chapter 5 this time
Sounds good. Same time, 7:30?
Perfect 👌
Good night everyone! See you tomorrow at the library 📚
Night! Don't forget to submit the assignment before midnight!
Already submitted ✅ Good night!
Submitting now... 🤞 Good night!
